note: Raw satellite/reanalysis products are not bundled because they are large and may require Earthdata/Copernicus authentication. Experiments record whether data were externally downloaded or whether literature-calibrated priors were used.

sources.name: NOAA/NSIDC CDR Passive Microwave Sea Ice Concentration v4
sources.access_note: Requires Earthdata/NSIDC download workflow; cache raw NetCDF outside git.
sources.intended_use: Regional SIC priors and Monte Carlo concentration distributions.

sources.name: NSIDC Sea Ice Concentrations from Nimbus-7 SMMR and DMSP SSM/I-SSMIS v2
sources.access_note: Use as cross-product uncertainty check against NOAA/NSIDC CDR.
sources.intended_use: Uncertainty envelope and sensitivity bounds for SIC.

sources.name: OSI SAF Global Sea Ice Concentration Climate Data Record v3
sources.authority: EUMETSAT OSI SAF / Norwegian Meteorological Institute
sources.access_note: Free registration at EUMETSAT. Alternative to NSIDC for cross-validation.
sources.intended_use: Cross-validation of SIC; European Arctic focus.

sources.name: CryoSat-2 Sea Ice Thickness L3
sources.access_note: Free via EUMETSAT Data Store. Covers Arctic winter only.
sources.intended_use: Ice thickness calibration for Lindqvist model and scenario priors.

sources.name: ICESat-2 ATL10 Sea Ice Freeboard
sources.access_note: Requires Earthdata account. Freeboard → thickness conversion needed.
sources.intended_use: High-resolution thickness validation and freeboard-to-thickness calibration.

sources.name: PIOMAS Pan-Arctic Ice Ocean Modeling and Assimilation System
sources.authority: University of Washington / Polar Science Center
sources.access_note: Free download from PSC website. No authentication required.
sources.intended_use: Long-term thickness climatology and trend analysis. Well-validated against ICESat/CryoSat.

sources.name: Copernicus Marine Arctic Ocean Sea Ice Reanalysis
sources.authority: Copernicus Marine Service / Mercator Ocean International
sources.access_note: Requires Copernicus Marine account/API client.
sources.intended_use: Ice SIC/SIT/drift calibration for scenarios B1-F1.

sources.name: Copernicus Marine Arctic Sea Ice Thickness Reprocessed L3
sources.access_note: Useful where CryoSat/Envisat thickness coverage exists; not year-round.
sources.intended_use: Thickness model calibration and seasonal uncertainty envelopes.

sources.name: NSIDC Polar Pathfinder Daily Sea Ice Motion Vectors v4
sources.access_note: Free via Earthdata. Combination of AVHRR, buoy, and passive microwave.
sources.intended_use: Ice drift speed/direction priors for scenario calibration. Core data for ice force model.

sources.name: IFREMER CERSAT Sea Ice Drift
sources.access_note: Free download. SSM/I and ASCAT based.
sources.intended_use: Cross-validation of ice drift; European Arctic and Antarctic coverage.

sources.name: OSCAR Ocean Surface Current Analyses Real-time
sources.access_note: Free via PO.DAAC Earthdata login.
sources.intended_use: Ocean current forcing for ice drift validation and DP station-keeping scenarios.

sources.name: GlobCurrent Global Ocean Currents
sources.access_note: Requires Copernicus Marine account.
sources.intended_use: Multi-source ocean current product for ice-ocean coupling.

sources.name: ERA5 Single Levels
sources.access_note: Requires CDS API key (free registration) and cdsapi Python package.
sources.intended_use: Wind/wave forcing for DP station-keeping; sea ice cover validation.

sources.name: JRA-55 Japanese 55-year Reanalysis
sources.access_note: Free download from JMA. Alternative to ERA5 for Arctic reanalysis.
sources.intended_use: Alternative atmospheric forcing; Arctic skill assessment.

sources.name: NIC Sea Ice Analysis (Daily & Weekly)
sources.access_note: Free download from NIC website. Shape/raster formats.
sources.intended_use: Operational ice chart for scenario validation; ice type classification.

sources.name: MASIE Ice Coverage (Daily)
sources.access_note: Free via NSIDC.
sources.intended_use: High-resolution daily ice extent for scenario boundary definition.

sources.name: N-ICE2015 Drifting Ice Station Data
sources.variable: ice_thickness, ice_strength, ice temperature, floe properties
sources.doi_or_product_id: Granskog et al., 2016 (BAMS); Itkin et al., 2017 (JGR)
sources.access_note: Free via Norwegian Polar Data Centre.
sources.intended_use: In-situ ice thickness/strength calibration for Lindqvist model. Key validation dataset.

sources.name: MOSAiC Multidisciplinary drifting Observatory for the Study of Arctic Climate
sources.variable: ice_thickness, ice_strength, ice temperature, snow depth, drift track
sources.access_note: Free registration at MOSAiC Data Portal. Most comprehensive Arctic ice dataset to date.
sources.intended_use: Full-cycle ice property calibration; drift speed distributions; ice strength validation.

sources.name: HSVA Ice Tank Test Data (Hamburg Ship Model Basin)
sources.authority: HSVA / Ship Design and Safety Lab
sources.access_note: Data typically obtained through research collaboration. Cite HSVA when using.
sources.intended_use: Ice force model calibration at model scale. Key for validating Lindqvist parameters.

sources.name: Aalto Ice Tank Test Data (Finland)
sources.authority: Aalto University / Kymenlaakso University of Applied Sciences
sources.access_note: Data obtained through research collaboration.
sources.intended_use: Ice force validation for Baltic/northern sea ice conditions.

sources.name: MarineTraffic / VesselFinder AIS Data
sources.access_note: Commercial API. Academic licenses available. Use for route validation only.
sources.intended_use: Arctic vessel route validation; DP operation pattern analysis.

sources.name: XueLong / XueLong2 Cruise Track Data
sources.access_note: Obtain through PRIC collaboration or published expedition reports.
sources.intended_use: Direct validation of DP scenarios in Arctic ice conditions.

sources.name: NSIDC Sea Ice Index Monthly Extent v4.0
sources.access_note: Free.
sources.intended_use: SIC climatology and trend.

literature_calibrations.notes: Uniaxial compressive strength of level ice. Varies with temperature, salinity, strain rate.
Empirical shape factor for ship-ice interaction. Depends on hull geometry.
Level ice compressive strength for Arctic structures. Conservative for ship interaction.
Consolidated ridge keel strength. For extreme ice scenarios.
Empirical ice pressure for ship transit in Baltic ice.

literature_calibrations.source: PRIC Technical Report / CSIC Ship Design

literature_calibrations.notes: Polar Class 7 (PC7), can transit 1.0m first-year ice at 5 knots.
DP3 station-keeping accuracy target in open water.
Typical watch circle for DP operations. Scenario D1 uses 12m.
